title: symplocos coronata var. coronata
intro: Symplocos coronata var. coronata, also known as the crowned syzygium or syringa, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of the Western Ghats in India. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine and is known for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which help in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Symplocos coronata var. coronata include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and bark are often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions to harness its medicinal properties, although its availability and usage may vary depending on the region.
